//: Deno toolchain for the supabase/functions edge functions (A+ Track F). The execution tests (test:edge) run in CI's deno-tests job. lint:edge + check:edge extend static analysis to the highest-trust code in the repo (auth/money trust boundary) — see scripts/validate-edge-functions.mjs for the source-syntax + botGuard contract gate that DOES run in CI today. Edge functions use full https://esm.sh/ URL imports, so no import map is needed. Run: `deno task test:edge` (or the CI `deno-tests` job).
//gate: ARMED: the money-path PRODUCTION sources are now type-checked in CI via the BLOCKING `deno task check:edge` step in the deno-tests job (stripe-webhook / create-checkout / generate-narrative + refundPolicy + requestMeta — these are green). test:edge KEEPS --no-check for now: with type-checking on, deno also checks the *.test.ts files, whose mock `adminClient`/`from()` stubs don't satisfy the full SupabaseClient<> type the handlers expect (9 TS2345 errors at time of writing). Drop --no-check here once those edge test mocks are typed to satisfy the handler deps (owned by the edge-function lane). lint:edge (non-blocking in CI) excludes the auto-generated _shared/*Bundle.js (built by scripts/build-edge-shared.mjs — not hand-written, not lintable).
//nmd: Edge functions are pure Deno (esm.sh / npm: imports resolved from the deno cache; no import map). Force nodeModulesDir=none so the deno-tests CI job — a clean runner with NO npm ci and NO node_modules — does not fall into node_modules mode and then fail to resolve the transitive @types/node that npm:stripe (and friends) pull in via type-reference directives. Without this, `deno check` errors with '@types/node is not installed' on the clean runner even though the sources are type-clean. See the deno-tests job in .github/workflows/ci.yml.
